     8→        "content": "     1→# Git Provider Service CLAUDE.md\n     2→\n     3→## Purpose\n     4→Provides GitLab REST API integration for automated merge request creation and management in transflow workflows.\n     5→\n     6→## Narrative Summary\n     7→The git provider service implements a clean interface for interacting with Git forge providers (currently GitLab, extensible to GitHub). It handles authentication, project URL parsing, merge request lifecycle management, and error handling. The service supports both creating new merge requests and updating existing ones based on source branch matching.\n     8→\n     9→The provider follows the principle of graceful degradation - MR creation failures do not break the parent workflow, allowing transformations to complete even when Git forge integration is unavailable.\n    10→\n    11→## Key Files\n    12→- `interface.go:5-26` - GitProvider interface and data structures\n    13→- `gitlab.go:14-272` - GitLab REST API implementation\n    14→- `gitlab.go:45-80` - Main CreateOrUpdateMR orchestration logic\n    15→- `gitlab.go:82-117` - Existing MR detection and conflict resolution\n    16→- `gitlab.go:218-250` - GitLab project URL parsing and validation\n    17→- `gitlab_test.go:1-150` - Mock implementation for testing\n    18→\n    19→## API Interface\n    20→### GitProvider Interface\n    21→- `CreateOrUpdateMR(ctx, config) -> (*MRResult, error)` - Primary MR management\n    22→- `ValidateConfiguration() -> error` - Pre-flight configuration check\n    23→\n    24→### Data Structures\n    25→- `MRConfig:12-19` - Input parameters for MR creation\n    26→- `MRResult:22-26` - Output result with URLs and metadata\n    27→\n    28→## Integration Points\n    29→### Consumes\n    30→- GitLab REST API: Projects, merge requests endpoints\n    31→- Environment variables: GITLAB_URL, GITLAB_TOKEN\n    32→\n    33→### Provides\n    34→- Merge request creation/updates for transflow workflows\n    35→- Project URL validation and parsing\n    36→- Authentication validation\n    37→\n    38→## Configuration\n    39→Required environment variables:\n    40→- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token with project access\n    41→- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL (optional, defaults to https://gitlab.com)\n    42→\n    43→Supported repository URL formats:\n    44→- `https://gitlab.example.com/namespace/project.git`\n    45→- `https://gitlab.com/group/subgroup/project.git`\n    46→\n    47→## Authentication Patterns\n    48→- Bearer token authentication via Authorization header\n    49→- Token validation on service initialization\n    50→- Graceful error handling for invalid/expired tokens\n    51→\n    52→## Key Patterns\n    53→- Interface-based design for provider extensibility\n    54→- URL parsing with validation (see gitlab.go:218-250)\n    55→- Idempotent operations - safe to retry MR creation\n    56→- Context-aware HTTP requests with timeout support\n    57→\n    58→## Related Documentation\n    59→- `../../cli/transflow/CLAUDE.md` - Main consumer service\n    60→- GitLab REST API documentation for merge requests endpoint\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

    18→        "content": "     1→---\n     2→task: h-implement-job-submission-infrastructure\n     3→branch: feature/implement-job-submission-infrastructure\n     4→status: completed\n     5→created: 2025-01-09\n     6→completed: 2025-01-09\n     7→modules: [internal/cli/transflow, internal/orchestration, api/arf]\n     8→---\n     9→\n    10→# Job Submission Infrastructure for Transflow MVP\n    11→\n    12→## Problem/Goal\n    13→\n    14→Implement the critical job submission and monitoring infrastructure required for transflow's LangGraph healing workflow. This is the foundational piece that enables:\n    15→- Planner job submission (LangGraph analysis of build failures)\n    16→- Parallel healing branch execution (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)\n    17→- Reducer job coordination (first-success-wins logic)\n    18→- Job status monitoring and artifact collection\n    19→\n    20→Without this infrastructure, the healing workflow cannot function and the MVP cannot be completed.\n    21→\n    22→## Success Criteria\n    23→\n    24→- [x] Implement `SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` for batch job submission and monitoring\n    25→- [x] Create orchestration helpers for planner/reducer/healing job types  \n    26→- [x] Add job status polling with proper timeout handling\n    27→- [x] Implement artifact collection from completed jobs\n    28→- [x] Write comprehensive unit tests for all job submission logic\n    29→- [x] Integration test with mock Nomad to validate workflow\n    30→- [x] Update `TransflowRunner` to use job submission for healing cycle\n    31→- [x] Document usage patterns and error handling\n    32→- [x] Pass all existing tests + new test coverage > 80%\n    33→- [x] Build compiles successfully (`go build ./...`)\n    34→- [x] Update CHANGELOG.md with implementation details\n    35→\n    36→## Context Files\n    37→\n    38→<!-- Core requirements from roadmap -->\n    39→- @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md                           # MVP specification and requirements\n    40→- @roadmap/transflow/orchestrator_submit_wait_terminal.md  # Job submission guidance\n    41→- @roadmap/transflow/orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md        # Parallel execution pattern\n    42→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/planner.hcl                    # Planner job template\n    43→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/reducer.hcl                    # Reducer job template\n    44→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/llm_exec.hcl                   # LLM execution job template\n    45→\n    46→<!-- Current implementation -->\n    47→- @internal/cli/transflow/runner.go                   # Main runner that needs job integration\n    48→- @internal/cli/transflow/run.go                      # CLI entry point with job flags\n    49→- @internal/orchestration/                            # Existing orchestration package\n    50→- @api/arf/multi_repo_orchestrator.go               # Reference for job patterns\n    51→\n    52→<!-- Test patterns -->\n    53→- @internal/cli/transflow/runner_test.go             # Existing test structure\n    54→- @AGENTS.md                                         # TDD requirements and testing guidelines\n    55→\n    56→## User Notes\n    57→\n    58→**Critical MVP Blocker**: This task represents the most critical missing piece for transflow MVP. All healing functionality depends on reliable job submission and monitoring.\n    59→\n    60→**TDD Approach Required**: Follow @AGENTS.md mandatory TDD cycle:\n    61→1. RED: Write failing tests for job submission scenarios\n    62→2. GREEN: Implement minimal working job submission logic  \n    63→3. REFACTOR: Test integration on VPS with real Nomad\n    64→\n    65→**Architecture Constraints**:\n    66→- Must integrate with existing `internal/orchestration` package\n    67→- Follow established patterns from ARF multi-repo orchestrator\n    68→- Support both synchronous (wait for completion) and asynchronous job execution\n    69→- Handle job timeouts, failures, and cancellation gracefully\n    70→\n    71→**Reference Implementation**: Use `orchestrator_submit_wait_terminal.md` and `orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md` as design guidance - these contain the architectural patterns already validated for the platform.\n    72→\n    73→## Context Manifest\n    74→\n    75→### How This Currently Works: Transflow Orchestration Architecture\n    76→\n    77→When a user initiates a transflow run via `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`, the request flows through a sophisticated multi-layer architecture that's designed around healing workflows and job orchestration.\n    78→\n    79→**Current Entry Point and Configuration Flow:**\n    80→The command enters through `internal/cli/transflow/run.go::runTransflow()`, which parses flags and loads the YAML configuration via `internal/cli/transflow/config.go::LoadConfig()`. The configuration system supports:\n    81→- Basic workflow definition (id, target_repo, base_ref, steps)\n    82→- Lane-specific deployment configuration (lane A-G auto-detection)\n    83→- Build timeout configuration with validation\n    84→- Self-healing configuration (max_retries, cooldown, enabled flag)\n    85→\n    86→**Current Implementation Status:**\n    87→The transflow system is **partially implemented** with several critical gaps:\n    88→- ✅ OpenRewrite recipe execution via ARF integration (`internal/cli/transflow/integrations.go`)\n    89→- ✅ Git operations wrapper around `api/arf/git_operations.go`\n    90→- ✅ Build checking via `SharedPush` from `internal/cli/common/deploy.go`  \n    91→- ✅ Basic runner structure with dependency injection (`internal/cli/transflow/runner.go`)\n    92→- ❌ **JOB SUBMISSION AND MONITORING INFRASTRUCTURE** (this task's focus)\n    93→- ❌ Healing workflow orchestration (planner → fanout → reducer)\n    94→- ❌ Artifact collection from completed jobs\n    95→\n    96→**Existing Orchestration Infrastructure:**\n    97→The system leverages `internal/orchestration/` package which provides:\n    98→\n    99→1. **Submit Functions** (`internal/orchestration/submit.go`):\n   100→   - `Submit(jobPath)` - Basic HCL job registration\n   101→   - `SubmitAndWaitHealthy()` - For services (not applicable to batch jobs)\n   102→   - **`SubmitAndWaitTerminal()`** - **ALREADY EXISTS** for batch jobs but needs integration\n   103→   - `ValidateJob()` and `PlanJob()` utilities\n   104→\n   105→2. **Monitoring Infrastructure** (`internal/orchestration/monitor.go`):\n   106→   - `HealthMonitor` with SDK adapter pattern\n   107→   - Job status polling with allocation state tracking\n   108→   - Terminal state detection (complete/failed) \n   109→   - Endpoint discovery for running services\n   110→\n   111→3. **Nomad SDK Integration** (`internal/orchestration/monitor_sdk_adapter.go`):\n   112→   - Clean abstraction over Nomad API client\n   113→   - Environment-driven configuration (`NOMAD_ADDR`)\n   114→   - Allocation status parsing with deployment health checks\n   115→\n   116→**Current Runner Architecture:**\n   117→The `TransflowRunner` in `internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` follows dependency injection pattern:\n   118→- Interface-based design (`GitOperationsInterface`, `RecipeExecutorInterface`, `BuildCheckerInterface`)\n   119→- Factory pattern via `TransflowIntegrations` in `integrations.go`\n   120→- Comprehensive test coverage with mock implementations in `runner_test.go`\n   121→\n   122→**Existing Job Templates and Patterns:**\n   123→The healing workflow architecture is defined through HCL job templates in `roadmap/transflow/jobs/`:\n   124→\n   125→1. **Planner Job** (`planner.hcl`):\n   126→   - LangGraph-based Docker container execution\n   127→   - Input: `inputs.json` with language, lane, last_error, deps\n   128→   - Output: `plan.json` with parallel healing options\n   129→   - Environment variables: MODEL, TOOLS_JSON, LIMITS_JSON, RUN_ID\n   130→   - Host volume mounts for context, kb (knowledge base), and output\n   131→\n   132→2. **Reducer Job** (`reducer.hcl`):  \n   133→   - Processes branch results and determines next actions\n   134→   - Input: `history.json` with plan_id, branches, winner\n   135→   - Output: `next.json` with action and notes\n   136→   - Lighter resource requirements (300 CPU, 512 MB memory)\n   137→\n   138→3. **Healing Branch Jobs**:\n   139→   - **LLM Exec** (`llm_exec.hcl`): Direct LLM-generated patch application\n   140→   - **ORW Apply** (`orw_apply.hcl`): OpenRewrite recipe execution\n   141→\n   142→All job templates use:\n   143→- Batch type with restart attempts = 0, mode = \"fail\"\n   144→- Host volume mounts for workspace isolation\n   145→- Environment variable placeholders for runtime substitution\n   146→- Consistent timeout patterns (5m kill_timeout, job-specific execution timeout)\n   147→\n   148→### For Job Submission Implementation: What Needs to Connect\n   149→\n   150→Since we're implementing the job submission infrastructure that enables healing workflows, this connects to the existing architecture at several critical integration points:\n   151→\n   152→**The TransflowRunner Integration Point:**\n   153→After a build failure in `runner.go::Run()`, the healing cycle needs to be triggered. Currently, the runner stops execution on build failure (lines 381-398), but the MVP requires planner job submission at this point. The architecture calls for:\n   154→\n   155→1. **Planner Submission** after build failure detection\n   156→2. **Plan Parsing** to extract healing options \n   157→3. **Fanout Execution** of parallel healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)\n   158→4. **First-Success-Wins** cancellation logic\n   159→5. **Reducer Job** to determine final actions\n   160→\n   161→**Command-Line Interface Extensions:**\n   162→The `run.go` already has partial job submission flags:\n   163→- `--plan` flag renders and optionally submits planner job\n   164→- `--exec-llm-first` and `--exec-orw-first` for branch execution\n   165→- `--apply-first` for diff application and validation\n   166→- Environment-driven submission control via `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT=1`\n   167→\n   168→**ARF System Integration Patterns:**\n   169→The existing `api/arf/openrewrite_dispatcher.go` demonstrates the job submission pattern we need to follow:\n   170→- Nomad client initialization with environment configuration\n   171→- Job template rendering with placeholder substitution  \n   172→- Error handling and retry logic\n   173→- Infrastructure validation before job submission\n   174→\n   175→**Volume and Artifact Management:**\n   176→The job templates expect host volume sources:\n   177→- `transflow-context` - Input data for jobs\n   178→- `transflow-kb` - Knowledge base for planner\n   179→- `transflow-out` - Artifact collection from completed jobs\n   180→- `transflow-history` - Branch execution results for reducer\n   181→\n   182→**Environment Variable Patterns:**\n   183→Jobs expect consistent environment configuration:\n   184→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` (default: \"gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06\")\n   185→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` (JSON allowlisting tools)  \n   186→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` (JSON with max_steps, max_tool_calls, timeout)\n   187→- Recipe-specific vars for ORW jobs (RECIPE_CLASS, RECIPE_COORDS)\n   188→\n   189→**Current Integration Gaps:**\n   190→The existing `SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` function provides the core job submission capability, but we need:\n   191→1. **Helper functions** for each job type (planner, reducer, llm-exec, orw-apply)\n   192→2. **Asset rendering** functions (already partially implemented in `runner.go`)\n   193→3. **Fanout orchestration** following the pseudo-code in `orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md`\n   194→4. **Artifact collection** after job completion\n   195→5. **Integration with TransflowRunner** healing cycle\n   196→\n   197→### Technical Reference Details\n   198→\n   199→#### Core Functions to Implement\n   200→\n   201→**Job Submission Helpers:**\n   202→```go\n   203→// In internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go\n   204→func SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext) (*PlannerResult, error)\n   205→func SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, history *BranchHistory) (*ReducerResult, error) \n   206→func SubmitLLMExecJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, option BranchSpec) (*BranchResult, error)\n   207→func SubmitORWApplyJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, option BranchSpec) (*BranchResult, error)\n   208→```\n   209→\n   210→**Fanout Orchestration:**\n   211→```go\n   212→// Following orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md pattern\n   213→func RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, plan []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (winner BranchResult, results []BranchResult, err error)\n   214→func executeBranch(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, spec BranchSpec, cancelCh <-chan struct{}) BranchResult\n   215→```\n   216→\n   217→#### Data Structures\n   218→\n   219→**Job Execution Context:**\n   220→```go\n   221→type RunContext struct {\n   222→    Config       *TransflowConfig\n   223→    WorkspaceDir string\n   224→    RepoPath     string\n   225→    OutDir       string\n   226→    JobTimeout   time.Duration\n   227→    BuildTimeout time.Duration\n   228→    Allowlist    []string\n   229→}\n   230→```\n   231→\n   232→**Branch Specifications (from fanout sketch):**\n   233→```go\n   234→type BranchSpec struct {\n   235→    ID     string\n   236→    Type   string // \"human\" | \"llm-exec\" | \"orw-gen\" \n   237→    Inputs map[string]any\n   238→}\n   239→\n   240→type BranchResult struct {\n   241→    ID         string\n   242→    Status     string // success|failed|canceled|timeout\n   243→    Artifact   string // diff.patch path\n   244→    JobName    string  \n   245→    JobID      string\n   246→    StartedAt  time.Time\n   247→    FinishedAt time.Time\n   248→    Notes      string\n   249→}\n   250→```\n   251→\n   252→#### Configuration Requirements\n   253→\n   254→**Environment Variables:**\n   255→- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad cluster address (orchestration package)\n   256→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` - LLM model specification\n   257→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` - Tool allowlist JSON\n   258→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` - Execution limits JSON\n   259→- `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT` - Enable actual job submission (vs dry-run)\n   260→\n   261→**Validation Schema (existing in transflow/schema.go):**\n   262→- `validatePlanJSON()` - Validates planner output structure\n   263→- `validateNextJSON()` - Validates reducer output structure\n   264→\n   265→#### File Locations\n   266→\n   267→**Implementation Structure:**\n   268→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go` - Core job submission helpers\n   269→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestration.go` - Healing workflow orchestration\n   270→- Extension: `internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` - Integrate healing cycle into main Run()\n   271→- Extension: `internal/orchestration/submit.go` - Add job-specific utilities if needed\n   272→\n   273→**Test Implementation:**\n   274→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission_test.go` - Unit tests for submission logic\n   275→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestration_test.go` - Integration tests for healing flow\n   276→- Extend: `internal/cli/transflow/runner_test.go` - Add healing workflow test cases\n   277→\n   278→**Job Template Integration:**\n   279→- Use existing: `roadmap/transflow/jobs/*.hcl` - Job templates with placeholder substitution\n   280→- Workspace structure: `${workspace}/planner/`, `${workspace}/llm-exec/${option-id}/`, etc.\n   281→\n   282→**Volume Mount Points:**\n   283→- Host volumes require Nomad cluster configuration\n   284→- Local development: Workspace subdirectories mounted as host volumes\n   285

content.thinking: Let me search for the SelfHealConfig in the actual transflow files more directly.
Let me check the self_healing.go file to understand the current SelfHealConfig structure.

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"errors"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/api/arf"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// SelfHealConfig represents the self-healing configuration in transflow.yaml
    13→type SelfHealConfig struct {
    14→	MaxRetries int    `yaml:"max_retries"`
    15→	Cooldown   string `yaml:"cooldown"`
    16→	Enabled    bool   `yaml:"enabled"`
    17→}
    18→
    19→// Validate checks if the self-healing configuration is valid
    20→func (c *SelfHealConfig) Validate() error {
    21→	if c.MaxRetries < 0 {
    22→		return errors.New("max_retries cannot be negative")
    23→	}
    24→	if c.MaxRetries > 5 {
    25→		return errors.New("max_retries cannot exceed 5")
    26→	}
    27→
    28→	if c.Cooldown != "" {
    29→		if _, err := time.ParseDuration(c.Cooldown); err != nil {
    30→			return fmt.Errorf("invalid cooldown format: %v", err)
    31→		}
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	return nil
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ParseCooldown parses the cooldown duration
    38→func (c *SelfHealConfig) ParseCooldown() (time.Duration, error) {
    39→	if c.Cooldown == "" {
    40→		return 0, nil
    41→	}
    42→	return time.ParseDuration(c.Cooldown)
    43→}
    44→
    45→// GetDefaults returns a default self-healing configuration
    46→func GetDefaultSelfHealConfig() *SelfHealConfig {
    47→	return &SelfHealConfig{
    48→		MaxRetries: 1,
    49→		Cooldown:   "",
    50→		Enabled:    true,
    51→	}
    52→}
    53→
    54→// TransflowHealingAttempt represents a single healing attempt in the transflow context
    55→// This is simpler than the complex ARF HealingAttempt and focused on transflow needs
    56→type TransflowHealingAttempt struct {
    57→	AttemptNumber    int              `json:"attempt_number"`
    58→	ErrorContext     arf.ErrorContext `json:"error_context"`
    59→	SuggestedRecipes []string         `json:"suggested_recipes"`
    60→	AppliedRecipes   []string         `json:"applied_recipes"`
    61→	Success          bool             `json:"success"`
    62→	ErrorMessage     string           `json:"error_message,omitempty"`
    63→	Duration         time.Duration    `json:"duration"`
    64→	Timestamp        time.Time        `json:"timestamp"`
    65→}
    66→
    67→// NewTransflowHealingAttempt creates a new healing attempt
    68→func NewTransflowHealingAttempt(attemptNumber int, errorContext arf.ErrorContext, suggestedRecipes []string) *TransflowHealingAttempt {
    69→	return &TransflowHealingAttempt{
    70→		AttemptNumber:    attemptNumber,
    71→		ErrorContext:     errorContext,
    72→		SuggestedRecipes: suggestedRecipes,
    73→		AppliedRecipes:   []string{},
    74→		Success:          false,
    75→		Timestamp:        time.Now(),
    76→	}
    77→}
    78→
    79→// MarkSuccess marks the healing attempt as successful
    80→func (a *TransflowHealingAttempt) MarkSuccess(appliedRecipes []string, duration time.Duration) {
    81→	a.Success = true
    82→	a.AppliedRecipes = appliedRecipes
    83→	a.Duration = duration
    84→	a.ErrorMessage = ""
    85→}
    86→
    87→// MarkFailure marks the healing attempt as failed
    88→func (a *TransflowHealingAttempt) MarkFailure(appliedRecipes []string, errorMsg string, duration time.Duration) {
    89→	a.Success = false
    90→	a.AppliedRecipes = appliedRecipes
    91→	a.ErrorMessage = errorMsg
    92→	a.Duration = duration
    93→}
    94→
    95→// TransflowHealingSummary tracks the overall healing process for a transflow run
    96→type TransflowHealingSummary struct {
    97→	Enabled       bool                       `json:"enabled"`
    98→	AttemptsCount int                        `json:"attempts_count"`
    99→	MaxRetries    int                        `json:"max_retries"`
   100→	Attempts      []*TransflowHealingAttempt `json:"attempts"`
   101→	FinalSuccess  bool                       `json:"final_success"`
   102→	TotalHealed   int                        `json:"total_healed"`
   103→	TotalDuration time.Duration              `json:"total_duration"`
   104→
   105→	// Job-based healing workflow fields
   106→	PlanID     string         `json:"plan_id,omitempty"`
   107→	Winner     *BranchResult  `json:"winner,omitempty"`
   108→	AllResults []BranchResult `json:"all_results,omitempty"`
   109→}
   110→
   111→// NewTransflowHealingSummary creates a new healing summary
   112→func NewTransflowHealingSummary(enabled bool, maxRetries int) *TransflowHealingSummary {
   113→	return &TransflowHealingSummary{
   114→		Enabled:       enabled,
   115→		AttemptsCount: 0,
   116→		MaxRetries:    maxRetries,
   117→		Attempts:      []*TransflowHealingAttempt{},
   118→		FinalSuccess:  false,
   119→		TotalHealed:   0,
   120→	}
   121→}
   122→
   123→// AddAttempt adds a healing attempt to the summary
   124→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) AddAttempt(attempt *TransflowHealingAttempt) {
   125→	s.Attempts = append(s.Attempts, attempt)
   126→	s.AttemptsCount++
   127→	s.TotalDuration += attempt.Duration
   128→
   129→	if attempt.Success {
   130→		s.TotalHealed++
   131→	}
   132→}
   133→
   134→// SetFinalResult sets the final success status
   135→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) SetFinalResult(success bool) {
   136→	s.FinalSuccess = success
   137→}
   138→
   139→// HasReachedMaxRetries checks if max retries have been reached
   140→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) HasReachedMaxRetries() bool {
   141→	return s.AttemptsCount >= s.MaxRetries
   142→}
   143→
   144→// BuildFailureAnalysis represents the analysis of build failure for recipe suggestions
   145→type BuildFailureAnalysis struct {
   146→	SuggestedRecipes []string `json:"suggested_recipes"`
   147→	Confidence       float64  `json:"confidence"`
   148→	ErrorType        string   `json:"error_type"`
   149→	Language         string   `json:"language,omitempty"`
   150→	SourceFiles      []string `json:"source_files,omitempty"`
   151→}
   152→
   153→// TransflowErrorAnalyzer analyzes build failures and suggests healing recipes
   154→type TransflowErrorAnalyzer struct {
   155→	// In the future, this might wrap the ARF analyzer
   156→	// For now, we'll use simple pattern matching
   157→}
   158→
   159→// NewTransflowErrorAnalyzer creates a new error analyzer for transflow
   160→func NewTransflowErrorAnalyzer() *TransflowErrorAnalyzer {
   161→	return &TransflowErrorAnalyzer{}
   162→}
   163→
   164→// AnalyzeBuildFailure analyzes build failure and suggests healing recipes
   165→func (a *TransflowErrorAnalyzer) AnalyzeBuildFailure(ctx context.Context, errors []string, language string) (*BuildFailureAnalysis, error) {
   166→	// For MVP, use simple error pattern matching to suggest common recipes
   167→	// In the future, integrate with the full ARF LLM analyzer
   168→
   169→	errorContext := arf.ExtractErrorContext(errors, language)
   170→
   171→	analysis := &BuildFailureAnalysis{
   172→		ErrorType:        errorContext.ErrorType,
   173→		Language:         language,
   174→		Confidence:       0.7, // Default confidence for pattern matching
   175→		SuggestedRecipes: []string{},
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	// Simple pattern-based recipe suggestions
   179→	switch errorContext.ErrorType {
   180→	case "import":
   181→		analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, "org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports")
   182→		analysis.Confidence = 0.8
   183→
   184→	case "compilation":
   185→		// Suggest common compilation fixes
   186→		if language == "java" {
   187→			analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes,
   188→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.SimplifyBooleanExpression",
   189→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses")
   190→		} else if language == "go" {
   191→			analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes,
   192→				"org.openrewrite.go.format.AutoFormat",
   193→				"org.openrewrite.go.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses")
   194→		}
   195→		analysis.Confidence = 0.6
   196→
   197→	case "dependency":
   198→		analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, "org.openrewrite.java.dependencies.DependencyVulnerabilityCheck")
   199→		analysis.Confidence = 0.5
   200→
   201→	default:
   202→		// For unknown errors, suggest general cleanup recipes
   203→		if language == "java" {
   204→			analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes,
   205→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.CommonStaticAnalysis")
   206→		}
   207→		analysis.Confidence = 0.4
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	// Extract source files if available
   211→	if errorContext.SourceFile != "" {
   212→		analysis.SourceFiles = []string{errorContext.SourceFile}
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	return analysis, nil
   216→}
   217→
